Sample Posts (Week 1 & 2 — Full Detail)

Below are 10 complete posts with captions, hashtags, and AI image prompts. The remaining 10 posts follow the same format and would be delivered in the full package.

WEEK 1
ML
Mountain Landscape Design
📅 Thu, May 1 · Instagram + Facebook · 🔄 Transformation
The snow finally melted and revealed... a mess. 😅 That's okay — that's what spring cleanups are for. Here's a typical Castle Rock backyard transformation — before and after a proper spring reset. A solid spring cleanup checklist: → Full debris and leaf removal → Edging along all beds and walkways → Fresh mulch application → Prune back winter-damaged shrubs → First mow of the season Total time for a typical yard: about 4 hours. The difference is night and day. 🌿 Ready to kick off your spring? Send us an email and we'll get you on the schedule.
#CastleRockLandscaping #SpringCleanup #BeforeAndAfter #CastleRockCO #LawnCare #CurbAppeal #CRLocalLove #ColoradoSpring #YardTransformation
📊 Best time: 12–2 PM 📝 Type: Carousel (before/after swipe)
ML
Mountain Landscape Design
📅 Mon, May 5 · Instagram + Facebook · 🎓 Education
Spring is HERE and we know you're itching to plant. But hold on — these 5 mistakes could cost you hundreds of dollars and weeks of wasted effort: 1️⃣ Planting too early. Colorado weather is sneaky. We can get frost into mid-May. Wait until nighttime temps are consistently above 40°F. 2️⃣ Ignoring your soil. Castle Rock soil is notoriously clay-heavy. Amend it before planting or your roots will struggle. 3️⃣ Overwatering new plants. More water ≠ more growth. Most new plants need deep watering 2-3x/week, not daily light sprinkles. 4️⃣ Forgetting about sun exposure. That south-facing bed gets INTENSE Colorado sun. Choose drought-tolerant varieties for those spots. 5️⃣ Skipping mulch. Mulch retains moisture, suppresses weeds, and insulates roots. It's the easiest win in landscaping. Save this post for when you're ready to plant! 📌 And if you'd rather have pros handle it — shoot us an email and we'll take it from there.
#GardeningTips #CastleRockGardening #SpringPlanting #ColoradoGardener #LandscapingTips #CRLocalLove #PlantingMistakes #YardGoals
📊 Best time: 2–4 PM Monday 📝 Type: Carousel (1 tip per slide)
ML
Mountain Landscape Design
📅 Wed, May 7 · Instagram + Facebook · 🎓 Education
What actually happens during a patio install? Here's a typical day 1: ☀️ → Demo the old concrete slab (usually done by mid-morning) → Grade and compact the base gravel → Set the edge restraints Most Castle Rock homeowners are surprised how fast it moves. A standard patio can go from demolition to finished pavers in about 3 days. The best part? Going from "someday we'll fix the backyard" to grilling on a brand new patio by the weekend. 👀
#BehindTheScenes #PatioInstall #CastleRockCO #HardscapeDesign #LandscapingLife #OutdoorLiving #PaverPatio #BackyardGoals
📊 Best time: 12–1 PM 📝 Type: Single photo or short video clip
ML
Mountain Landscape Design
📅 Fri, May 9 · Instagram + Facebook · 🏔️ Community
Weekend plans? 🌿 The Castle Rock Farmer's Market kicks off this Saturday at Festival Park! Local produce, live music, food trucks, and a whole lot of Castle Rock community. It's one of the best places to grab plants, support local vendors, and connect with your neighbors. Pro tip: if you're picking up annuals at the market, wait until mid-May to plant them in the ground. Colorado springs are unpredictable. Grab them now, keep them in a sunny spot indoors, and transplant once frost risk passes. See you there, Castle Rock! 🤝
#CastleRock #CRLocalLove #CastleRockFarmersMarket #SupportLocalCR #ShopLocal #CastleRockCommunity #ColoradoWeekend
📊 Best time: 11 AM–1 PM Friday 📝 Type: Single photo
ML
Mountain Landscape Design
📅 Sat, May 10 · Instagram Reel · 🔄 Transformation
3 days. 1 patio. 0 regrets. 🔥 This is what a typical patio transformation looks like — from old cracked concrete to a paver patio built for Colorado living. What goes into a quality patio build: → Belgard Catalina Slate pavers (or similar) → Polymeric sand joints (locks pavers tight, prevents weeds) → Integrated LED step lighting → Built-in fire pit pad The turnaround from "demolition" to "dinner party" is usually about 3 days. Not bad. 😏 If you've been thinking about upgrading your outdoor space, drop us an email — summer booking fills up fast in Castle Rock.
#PatioTransformation #BeforeAndAfter #CastleRockLandscaping #PaverPatio #OutdoorLiving #BackyardMakeover #ColoradoLiving #Timelapse
📊 Best time: 9–11 AM Saturday 📝 Type: Reel (15-30 sec timelapse) + carousel
WEEK 2
ML
Mountain Landscape Design
📅 Mon, May 12 · Instagram + Facebook · 🎓 Education
🌵 DROUGHT-FRIENDLY PLANTS
That Thrive in Castle Rock
Colorado gets ~17 inches of rain a year. Your garden needs to work with that, not against it. Here are our top 5 drought-friendly plants that look incredible in Castle Rock yards: 🌿 Russian Sage — purple blooms, practically indestructible 🌸 Lavender — gorgeous, fragrant, and deer-resistant 🌼 Black-Eyed Susan — pops of gold all summer long 🪴 Blue Avena Grass — movement and texture with zero fuss 🌺 Penstemon — Colorado native, hummingbird magnet All of these need minimal watering once established and thrive in our alkaline clay soil. Want a xeriscape plan designed for your specific yard? Email us and we'll put one together for you. 🌱
#Xeriscape #DroughtTolerant #CastleRockGardening #ColoradoPlants #WaterWise #CRLocalLove #LandscapeDesign #NativePlants
📊 Best time: 2–4 PM Monday 📝 Type: Carousel (1 plant per slide)
ML
Mountain Landscape Design
📅 Wed, May 14 · Instagram + Facebook · 💰 Promotion
💰 SPRING SPECIAL
10% Off New Projects
Spring is prime booking season for landscaping projects. 🌷 If you've been thinking about a patio, retaining wall, full yard design, or xeriscape conversion — now is the time to start getting quotes. Here's why spring matters: ✅ Best weather window for installation ✅ Landscapers still have availability before summer rush ✅ Plants establish better with spring rain and cool nights Most Castle Rock landscapers are booked through August by mid-June. The ones who plan early get the best crews and the best pricing. Don't wait until your neighbors are already enjoying their new backyard. 😎 📧 Email us to schedule your free consultation.
#SpringSpecial #CastleRockLandscaping #LandscapeDesign #OutdoorLiving #CRLocalLove #BackyardGoals #FreeConsultation
📊 Best time: 12–2 PM Wednesday 📝 Type: Single graphic
ML
Mountain Landscape Design
📅 Thu, May 15 · Instagram + Facebook · 🔄 Transformation
From "we never use the backyard" to "we're out here every night." 🔥 Sound familiar? A lot of Castle Rock homes have huge backyards that are basically just grass and a broken fence. All that space, no reason to go out there. Here's what a typical backyard transformation includes: → Custom stone fire pit with seating wall → Flagstone patio surround → Low-voltage landscape lighting → Native plantings along the perimeter → New cedar privacy fence A project like this usually takes about 8 days. The result? Kids who won't come inside. 😂 Every backyard has potential. Sometimes it just takes a fresh set of eyes to see it. Email us for a free walkthrough.
#FirePit #BackyardTransformation #CastleRockCO #OutdoorLiving #LandscapeDesign #BeforeAndAfter #ColoradoBackyard #CurbAppeal
📊 Best time: 12–2 PM Thursday 📝 Type: Carousel (before + process + after)
ML
Mountain Landscape Design
📅 Sat, May 17 · Instagram Carousel · 🎓 Education
💧 IRRIGATION 101
When & How Long to Water
Castle Rock water restrictions start June 1. Here's how to keep your lawn green without wasting a drop: 💧 WHEN to water: → Before 10 AM or after 6 PM (less evaporation) → Follow your assigned watering days (Castle Rock uses odd/even system) → Skip watering after rain — seems obvious but most sprinkler systems don't know it's raining 💧 HOW LONG: → Cool-season grass (Kentucky Bluegrass): 20-30 min per zone, 2-3x per week → Warm-season grass (Buffalo grass): 15-20 min, 1-2x per week → New sod: Daily for first 2 weeks, then taper down → Established xeriscape: Once per week or less 💧 PRO TIP: Put a tuna can in your yard while sprinklers run. When it's full (~1 inch), that zone is done. Cheapest irrigation audit there is. Save this for June! 📌
#IrrigationTips #WaterWise #CastleRockCO #LawnCare #ColoradoGardening #SprinklerSystem #WaterConservation #CRLocalLove
📊 Best time: 9–11 AM Saturday 📝 Type: Carousel (3-4 slides, 1 section each)
ML
Mountain Landscape Design
📅 Mon, May 19 · Instagram + Facebook · 🎓 Education
How to pick the right landscaper in Castle Rock 🤝 Not all crews are the same. Here's what to look for before you hire: → Do they have photos of completed local projects? (Colorado conditions are unique) → Are they licensed and insured in Douglas County? → Will the same crew start and finish your project? → Do they guarantee their hardscape work for at least 2 years? → Can they provide references from Castle Rock homeowners? A great landscaper isn't just someone with a truck and a shovel. It's someone who takes pride in every stone they lay and treats your yard like their own. Ask the right questions upfront and you'll save yourself headaches later. Need a recommendation? Email us. 💪
#HiringTips #CastleRockBusiness #LandscapingTips #SmallBusinessSupport #CRLocalLove #HardscapeDesign #ColoradoLandscaping
📊 Best time: 2–4 PM Monday 📝 Type: Carousel (1 tip per slide)

Hashtag Strategy

Each post uses 8-10 hashtags following a 3-tier approach for maximum local reach:

Tier 1: Location (always included)

#CastleRockCO #CRLocalLove #CastleRockLandscaping #SupportCastleRock

Tier 2: Industry (rotate based on post topic)

#LandscapeDesign #OutdoorLiving #CurbAppeal #BackyardGoals #HardscapeDesign #Xeriscape

Tier 3: Content-Specific (match post type)

#BeforeAndAfter #SpringCleanup #DroughtTolerant #PatioDesign #FirePit #LawnCare

We keep total hashtags under 10 per post. Research shows 3-5 highly relevant hashtags outperform larger sets. Local hashtags drive 3x more engagement from nearby customers than generic ones.

How to Post This Content

Every post in this package is ready to copy, paste, and publish. Here's exactly how to do it — takes about 2 minutes per post.

Instagram

Open Instagram and tap the + button

Select "Post" from the menu at the bottom of your screen.

Add your image

Use the AI image prompt listed on the post to generate an image (we recommend ChatGPT or Canva's AI tool), or use a photo from your camera roll that matches the description.

Paste the caption

Copy the full caption text from the post card below. Paste it into the caption field. The line breaks are already formatted.

Add hashtags

Copy the hashtag set listed under each post and paste it at the end of your caption, or in the first comment.

Post at the recommended time

Each post card shows the best posting time. Schedule it using Instagram's built-in scheduler (tap "Advanced settings" → "Schedule") or post it live.

Facebook

Go to your Business Page

Make sure you're posting from your business page, not your personal profile. Tap your profile icon and switch to your page.

Create a new post

Tap "Create post" at the top of your page. Add the same image you used for Instagram.

Paste the caption

Same caption works for both platforms. Facebook doesn't need hashtags — you can skip them or include 2–3 of the location tags.

Schedule or publish

Click the dropdown arrow next to "Post" and select "Schedule post" to set the recommended time, or publish immediately.

Pro tip: Batch your posting. Set aside 30 minutes on a Sunday to schedule the entire week. Most of our clients spend less than 2 hours per month posting all 20 pieces of content.
